dimensions.name: Developer experience and delivery supply chain

controls.text: System drivers, risks, and measurable quality attributes govern architecture choices.
controls.critical_when: An unknown driver can change topology, trust, recovery, or a critical-journey target.

controls.text: Repository and deployment topology are explicit, and every stronger boundary has evidence.
controls.critical_when: A split changes failure isolation, data ownership, deployment, or on-call responsibility.

controls.text: Modules align to business capabilities and expose stable public APIs.
controls.critical_when: A module owns money, entitlement, regulated data, or a shared critical workflow.

controls.text: Dependency direction, cycles, runtime separation, and public surfaces are mechanically checked.
controls.critical_when: A violation can bypass a trust boundary or corrupt write ownership.

controls.text: Commands, queries, DTOs, errors, transactions, and compatibility policies have owners.
controls.critical_when: The contract has external, cross-module, or independently deployed consumers.

controls.text: Cross-module flows use public contracts and preserve authoritative write ownership.
controls.critical_when: The flow crosses tenant, money, inventory, or irreversible-state boundaries.

controls.text: Transport, application, domain, and adapter shapes are translated deliberately.
controls.critical_when: An external or storage shape can leak privileged fields or volatile semantics.

controls.text: Composition roots wire concrete adapters outside business policy.
controls.critical_when: Hidden runtime lookup could select a privileged or production dependency.

controls.text: Material trust boundaries have a current threat and abuse model.
controls.critical_when: The system is internet-facing, privileged, multi-tenant, regulated, or handles sensitive assets.

controls.text: Authentication, session lifecycle, and default-deny resource authorization are server enforced.
controls.critical_when: Any non-public resource or action is callable.

controls.text: Identity, session, secret, and personal-data lifecycles have owners and end states.
controls.critical_when: Credentials, personal data, privileged identity, or recovery paths exist.

controls.text: Negative tests prove horizontal, vertical, tenant, and sensitive-data denial paths.
controls.critical_when: Ownership, roles, support access, or tenancy affects authorization.

controls.text: Authoritative and sensitive data copies have classification, ownership, retention, and deletion rules.
controls.critical_when: Data is sensitive, regulated, business-critical, or copied to vendors/backups.

controls.text: Schema and data changes use compatible phases and resumable observable backfills.
controls.critical_when: Existing durable data or mixed application versions are affected.

controls.text: Integrity, environment/tenant isolation, query ownership, and migration order are explicit.
controls.critical_when: Wrong-scope access or write order can corrupt or disclose data.

controls.text: Restore drills prove declared RPO and RTO across authoritative stores.
controls.critical_when: Durable state cannot be reconstructed safely from another authority.

controls.text: Remote and concurrent operations have deadlines, cancellation, retry, idempotency, and invariant protection.
controls.critical_when: A timeout, duplicate, or concurrent call can create an irreversible effect.

controls.text: Durable asynchronous work has identity, state, duplicate handling, and recovery semantics.
controls.critical_when: A queue, scheduler, event, worker, or resumable job is selected.

controls.text: Vendor and webhook protocols separate authenticity, delivery state, policy, and reconciliation.
controls.critical_when: An external provider can cause or confirm a business effect.

controls.text: Ordering, quarantine, replay, partial failure, and degraded modes have observable recovery paths.
controls.critical_when: Lost, reordered, or replayed work can violate a critical journey.

controls.text: Server, browser, streaming, synchronous, and asynchronous paths are selected deliberately.
controls.critical_when: Runtime choice changes trust, secrets, latency, or connection behavior.

controls.text: Native platform capability precedes wrappers and preserves semantic accessibility.
controls.critical_when: A wrapper changes input, focus, keyboard, error, or upgrade behavior.

controls.text: Critical journeys have representative accessibility and performance budgets.
controls.critical_when: The journey has a contractual accessibility or latency target.

controls.text: Capacity, saturation, cold start, query, bundle, and cost claims use representative evidence.
controls.critical_when: Traffic, cardinality, quotas, or cost can exhaust a shared resource.

controls.text: Test types map to failure modes at the lowest sufficient evidence layer.
controls.critical_when: A material failure mode has no cheaper deterministic proof.

controls.text: Domain, application, repository, contract, UI, async, migration, and nonfunctional risks have owners.
controls.critical_when: A substitute cannot reproduce the contract being claimed.

controls.text: Test data is isolated, valid, non-sensitive, and deterministic for time, IDs, locale, and concurrency.
controls.critical_when: A test can touch shared, sensitive, or production-like durable state.

controls.text: Architecture invariants and brownfield baselines are automated and ratcheted.
controls.critical_when: A cheap mechanical violation can bypass a critical boundary.

controls.text: Clone-to-running, check, build, reset, and evidence discovery are reproducible.
controls.critical_when: Only one person or ambient machine state can reproduce the release.

controls.text: CI treats untrusted contributions safely and enforces deterministic gates.
controls.critical_when: CI can access secrets, artifacts, infrastructure, or deploy authority.

controls.text: Reviewed artifacts are immutable or reproducibly rebuilt with distinct provenance.
controls.critical_when: A release reaches production or a regulated/customer-controlled environment.

controls.text: Deploy, migration, rollout, rollback/roll-forward, and compatibility windows are rehearsable.
controls.critical_when: Old and new application, data, event, or worker versions coexist.

controls.text: Logs, metrics, and traces correlate critical request and workflow paths safely.
controls.critical_when: A critical failure cannot be diagnosed from one correlated path.

controls.text: Critical capabilities have owned SLIs, SLOs, alerts, health semantics, and runbooks.
controls.critical_when: Availability, latency, or recovery is a declared system baseline.

controls.text: Incident detection, communication, mitigation, recovery, and follow-up are exercised.
controls.critical_when: Failure can materially affect users, security, data, or contractual service.

controls.text: Telemetry cardinality, sampling, retention, access, privacy, drop behavior, and cost are governed.
controls.critical_when: Telemetry can leak sensitive data or exhaust production resources/budget.

controls.text: Costly decisions and bounded exceptions have owners, evidence, expiry, and revisit triggers.
controls.critical_when: A decision is costly to reverse or accepts material residual risk.

controls.text: Public contracts have compatibility, deprecation, consumer migration, and decommission paths.
controls.critical_when: A consumer cannot be upgraded atomically or usage cannot be observed.

controls.text: Refactors characterize behavior and migrate through an explicit measurable seam.
controls.critical_when: Current behavior or data cannot change silently.

controls.text: Repo guidance routes humans and agents to minimal current owners and verified evidence.
controls.critical_when: Stale or conflicting guidance can authorize a high-risk implementation.
